flashcards.front: What is the main goal of parallel programming?
flashcards.back: To decrease execution time and increase computational throughput by using multiple processing units to work on different parts of a problem simultaneously.
Multiple Instruction, Multiple Data. An architecture where multiple processors execute different instruction streams on different data streams. (e.g., multicore CPUs, computer clusters).

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between UMA and NUMA?
flashcards.back: UMA (Uniform Memory Access): Memory access time is equal for all processors. NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access): Memory access time depends on the memory location relative to the processor.

flashcards.front: What is the cache coherence problem?
flashcards.back: The problem that arises when multiple caches hold copies of the same data, and one processor updates its copy, making the other copies stale and inconsistent.

flashcards.front: Name one advantage and one disadvantage of a distributed-memory system.
flashcards.back: Advantage: Highly scalable. Disadvantage: More complex to program due to explicit message passing.
